summary.brief: Swanigan v. Avenues Healthcare. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: Swanigan resided in a long-term healthcare facility operated by Avenues Healthcare Inc. (Avenues) from 2009 through 2013.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In early 2013, Swanigan “developed cuts or other injuries . . . to both of his feet.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Around October 2013, Swanigan developed infections in both feet, which required his transfer to the University of Utah Medical Center for treatment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Physicians at the hospital determined that both of Swanigan’s feet would have to be amputated.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Swanigan died from a heart attack.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Swanigan suffered from coronary disease and hypertension, smoked heavily, was obese, and led a sedentary lifestyle.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Expert’s testimony largely consisted of the assertion that Swanigan’s infection resulted in inflammation, which in turn resulted in coronary problems.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: The district court determined that the expert testimony Swanigan’s family sought to introduce in this respect was unreliable and, on that basis, excluded that testimony from trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court then determined that, without the excluded testimony, Swanigan’s estate could not prove its claim, and therefore it granted summary judgment in favor of the nursing home.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The testimony of [Expert] fails to meet the requirements for admissible testimony under rules 702(b) or 702(c) of the Utah Rules of Evidence.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: There is no dispute that [Expert’s] opinion is not based on general acceptance in the relevant expert community.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Estate had “no expert testimony to support its claims for medical malpractice or wrongful death.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court did not abuse its discretion in striking Expert’s opinion.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Developed cuts or other injuries to both feet in early 2013, refused medical care on numerous occasions
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Infections in both feet requiring medical intervention

requestables.name: Hospital Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation at University of Utah Medical Center

reveal.result_summary: Determined both feet would have to be amputated

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with foot injuries and infections

timeline_phases.name: Hospital Workup
timeline_phases.description: Patient transferred to hospital for further evaluation

decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.clinical_reasoning: To prevent further complications from infection
Patient refused care, leading to delay

actual_action_defendant.description: Surgery was delayed due to patient's refusal of care

expected_action_court.description: Immediate surgical intervention should have been pursued

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Delay in surgical intervention led to worsening condition
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care for severe infections

patient_outcome.description: Patient died from a heart attack before surgery

key_findings: Expert testimony excluded, no causation proven
Summary judgment in favor of nursing home

factual_timeline.event: Swanigan developed cuts or other injuries to both feet
Swanigan developed infections in both feet

treatments.outcome: Not performed due to patient's death

allegations.allegation: Failure to provide adequate care leading to infection

court_findings.finding: No expert testimony to support causation
